perturbation.original_text: 1.6 HDI shall have the Site fully operational and accessible on demand by users of the Internet no later than sixty (60) days from the date this Agreement has been executed by both Parties. In the event that HDI fails to have the Site fully operational within seventy (70) days from the date of this Agreement, the Company shall have the right to terminate this Agreement without penalty.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.6 HDI shall use its best efforts to have the Site operational and accessible on demand by users of the Internet within a reasonable timeframe, estimated to be sixty (60) days from the date this Agreement has been executed by both Parties. The Company's right to terminate for failure to launch within seventy (70) days is subject to reasonable delays caused by unforeseen circumstances, and termination will only be permitted if the delay is deemed unreasonable.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall have' to 'shall use its best efforts' introduces ambiguity and weakens HDI's obligation. The original contract grants the Company the right to terminate without penalty if the site isn't operational within 70 days. By adding a clause for 'reasonable delays caused by unforeseen circumstances' and requiring the delay to be deemed unreasonable, this gives HDI more leeway in how/when the site can be deemed operational. The original language in Section 1.6, is now contradicted, since is not specific and the operational time is not clearly defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.6 HDI shall use its best efforts to have the Site operational and accessible on demand by users of the Internet within a reasonable timeframe, estimated to be sixty (60) days from the date this Agreement has been executed by both Parties. The Company's right to terminate for failure to launch within seventy (70) days is subject to reasonable delays caused by unforeseen circumstances, and termination will only be permitted if the delay is deemed unreasonable.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a firm deadline (60/70 days) for the site to be operational, with a clear right to terminate for failure to meet this deadline. The changed text introduces ambiguity ('best efforts', 'reasonable timeframe', 'reasonable delays') and weakens the termination clause. This directly contradicts the original intent of the contract, as it introduces uncertainty about when the site must be operational and under what circumstances the company can terminate. The original text provided a specific obligation, while the changed text replaces it with a more subjective standard, hence a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2 The Company shall have exclusive artistic and editorial control over the Site, including, without limitation, the implementation of the Content on the Site and the design and look and feel of the Site. Neither the Site nor any portion of thereof shall be deemed accepted and approved by the Company unless and until the Company accepts and approves same in writing to HDI. No portion of the Site shall be made available on the Internet without the consent of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 The Company shall have significant artistic and editorial input regarding the Site, including, without limitation, the implementation of the Content on the Site and the design and look and feel of the Site. HDI will consider the Company's input, but retains ultimate authority over the final design and functionality of the Site. The Site shall be deemed accepted and approved by the Company once it has been live for 30 days without written objections being raised by the Company to HDI. No portion of the Site shall be subject to consent from the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'exclusive artistic and editorial control' to 'significant artistic and editorial input' but ultimate authority with HDI creates a direct contradiction. The original contract states that the Company must accept the site in writing before launch. The changed version removes the need for written acceptance by saying that the site is deemed approved automatically after being live for 30 days. Also by saying that no portion of the Site, shall be subject to consent from the Company. It is also contradicting the original sentence, where nothing can be online without the consent of the Company.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2 The Company shall have significant artistic and editorial input regarding the Site, including, without limitation, the implementation of the Content on the Site and the design and look and feel of the Site. HDI will consider the Company's input, but retains ultimate authority over the final design and functionality of the Site. The Site shall be deemed accepted and approved by the Company once it has been live for 30 days without written objections being raised by the Company to HDI. No portion of the Site shall be subject to consent from the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original contract states the Company's 'exclusive artistic and editorial control' and requires written acceptance before launch. The revised version grants HDI 'ultimate authority' and deems the site approved automatically after 30 days of being live, contradicting the requirement for written acceptance and 'exclusive' control. It also includes a clause saying no part of the site shall be subject to consent from the Company, thus creating a contradiction.
